frameworks.name: Gaussian Process regression with diffusion kernel
frameworks.one_line: GP model with graph diffusion kernel captures generalization over connected reward structures

frameworks.name: function learning
frameworks.one_line: psychological function-learning models extended to graph-structured input spaces

frameworks.name: Successor Representation
frameworks.one_line: reinforcement-learning SR linked to GP-diffusion generalization in graphs

frameworks.name: multi-armed bandit
frameworks.one_line: exploration-exploitation paradigm extended onto a graph topology

core_question: How do people generalize and explore reward structures defined over graph-connected spaces?
key_assumptions: generalization can be modeled with a kernel over the graph
human choices reflect learned value plus uncertainty
manipulations: connectivity structure of the underlying reward graph
bandit choice options embedded in graph nodes
key_findings: a GP with diffusion kernel best describes participant choices among compared models
the same model predicts expected-reward judgments and confidence ratings
interpretations: graph-diffusion GP unifies function learning with Successor Representation
human generalization in structured spaces leverages connectivity-based similarity
